title: The Arrow of Gold
description: The Arrow of Gold — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "The Arrow of Gold" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.label: "Author’s Note"
memes.description: "Having named all the short prefaces written for my books, Author’s Notes, this one too must have the same heading for the sake of uniformity if at the risk of some confusion."

memes.label: "Colophon"
memes.description: "The Arrow of Gold was published in 1919 by Joseph Conrad. This ebook was produced for Standard Ebooks by Szymon Szott, and is based on a transcription produced in 1997 by"

memes.label: "First Note"
memes.description: "The pages which follow have been extracted from a pile of manuscript which was apparently meant for the eye of one woman only. She seems to have been the writer’s childhood’s friend."

memes.label: "Imprint"
memes.description: "This ebook is the product of many hours of hard work by volunteers for Standard Ebooks, and builds on the hard work of other literature lovers made possible by the public domain."

memes.label: "Second Note"
memes.description: "The narrative of our man goes on for some six months more, from this, the last night of the Carnival season up to and beyond the season of roses."

memes.label: "Table of Contents"
memes.description: "Table of Contents for The Arrow of Gold"

memes.label: "The Arrow of Gold"
memes.description: "Title page and subtitle of the novel."

memes.label: Adventure
memes.description: The narrative mode through which the protagonist's youthful experience of Carlist gun-running is framed, presented by Conrad as a historically terminal form of romantic action

memes.label: Allègre's Art Collection
memes.description: The extraordinary collection of art and precious objects assembled by Henry Allègre, inherited by Doña Rita, forming the material environment of the novel's world

memes.label: The Alphonsist Forces
memes.description: The liberal government forces opposing the Carlist insurrection, named for Alfonso XII — the rival claimant whose shells provide the novel's most vivid wartime imagery

memes.label: Azzolati
memes.description: A minor figure in Doña Rita's circle whose comic-heartbroken devotion to her provides a lighter counterpoint to the novel's more dangerous romantic entanglements

memes.label: Azzolati's Dinner
memes.description: Doña Rita's strategic dinner with the international banker Azzolati at her Paris hotel, intended to secure diplomatic access through him, which ends in her throwing him out after he reveals a brutal contempt for the poor.

memes.label: Baron H.
memes.description: A Carlist agent operating between Marseilles and the Quartel Real in Tolosa, whose messages and arrangements connect the novel's French setting to the Spanish military operations

memes.label: The Basque Hills
memes.description: The rural Basque country of northern Spain, Doña Rita's homeland and the landscape of her youth among the hills and gorges

memes.label: Bastia
memes.description: Corsican port town to which Dominic travels by mail-boat to procure the balancelle — the fast sailing vessel essential to the Carlist gun-running operation.

memes.label: Bay of Biscay
memes.description: Body of water off the Atlantic coast of France where Mills's supply steamer was shipwrecked — the maritime disaster that sets the Carlist arms-smuggling plot in motion.

memes.label: Bayonne
memes.description: The French border town near the Spanish frontier, a key transit point for Carlist agents and arms smuggling operations

memes.label: Blunt's Duel with George
memes.description: The pistol duel between Captain Blunt and Monsieur George in a walled garden near Marseilles, fought over Doña Rita, in which both men are gravely wounded

memes.label: The Cannebière
memes.description: The grand boulevard of Marseilles — "If Paris had a Cannebière it would be a little Paris" — where the novel's bohemian, political, and maritime worlds intersect, and where Monsieur George's circle meets daily on the pavement opposite the Bourse.

memes.label: Captain Blunt
memes.description: The charming American adventurer and former Confederate officer whose rivalry with Monsieur George for Doña Rita's attention introduces danger and desperation into the novel's romantic geometry

memes.label: Carlism
memes.description: The political movement supporting Don Carlos de Bourbon's claim to the Spanish throne, forming the historical backdrop of the novel's gun-running plot

memes.label: The Carlist Army
memes.description: The military forces fighting for Don Carlos de Bourbon in the Third Carlist War — the intended recipient of the arms smuggled by the novel's protagonists

memes.label: The Carlist Organization
memes.description: The political-administrative structure of the Carlist cause in the 1870s — the committee, headquarters, and enterprise that orchestrate the conspiracy driving the novel's plot

memes.label: Ceuta
memes.description: Spanish penal colony on the North African coast, referenced by Dominic as the punishment awaiting those caught in the Carlist smuggling trade — a marker of the real stakes of the conspirators' unlawful enterprise.

memes.label: "Part I, Chapter I"
memes.description: "Certain streets have an atmosphere of their own, a sort of universal fame and the particular affection of their citizens."

memes.label: "Part I, Chapter II"
memes.description: "The street in which Mr. Blunt lived presented itself to our eyes, narrow, silent, empty, and dark, but with enough gas-lamps in it to disclose its most striking feature: a quantity of flagpoles sti..."

memes.label: "Part I, Chapter III"
memes.description: "Mills lowered the hands holding the extinct and even cold pipe before his big face. “H’m, shoot an arrow into that old man’s heart like this? But was there anything done?”"

memes.label: "Part II, Chapter I"
memes.description: "Sometimes I wonder yet whether Mills wished me to oversleep myself or not: that is, whether he really took sufficient interest to care."

memes.label: "Part II, Chapter II"
memes.description: "For this, properly speaking wonderful, reason I was the only one of the company who could listen without constraint to the unbidden guest with that fine head of white hair, so beautifully kept, so ..."

memes.label: "Part II, Chapter III"
memes.description: "Mills got up and approached the figure at the window. To my extreme surprise, Mr. Blunt, after a moment of obviously painful hesitation, hastened out after the man with the white hair."

memes.label: "Part II, Chapter IV"
memes.description: "It was past four o’clock before I left the house, together with Mills. Mr. Blunt, still in his riding costume, escorted us to the very door."

memes.label: "Part III, Chapter I"
memes.description: "It was on our return from that first trip that I took Dominic up to the Villa to be presented to Doña Rita."

memes.label: "Part III, Chapter II"
memes.description: "Doña Rita was curious to know how I got on with her peasant sister and all I could say in return for that inquiry was that the peasant sister was in her own way amiable."

memes.label: "Part III, Chapter III"
memes.description: "On our return from that expedition we came gliding into the old harbour so late that Dominic and I, making for the café kept by Madame Léonore, found it empty of customers, except for two rather si..."

memes.label: "Part III, Chapter IV"
memes.description: "That night I passed in a state, mostly open-eyed, I believe, but always on the border between dreams and waking. The only thing absolutely absent from it was the feeling of rest."

memes.label: "Part IV, Chapter I"
memes.description: "“Such a charming lady in a grey silk dress and a hand as white as snow. She looked at me through such funny glasses on the end of a long handle."

memes.label: "Part IV, Chapter II"
memes.description: "The windows of that room gave out on the street of the Consuls which as usual was silent. And the house itself below me and above me was soundless, perfectly still."

memes.label: "Part IV, Chapter III"
memes.description: "Without caring much about it I was conscious of sudden illumination. I said to myself confidently that these two people had been quarrelling all the morning."

memes.label: "Part IV, Chapter IV"
memes.description: "Through the great arched window of the hall I saw the hotel brougham waiting at the door. On passing the door of the front room (it was originally meant for a drawing-room but a bed for Blunt was p..."

memes.label: "Part IV, Chapter V"
memes.description: "I took my eyes from her face and became aware that dusk was beginning to steal into the room. How strange it seemed."

memes.label: "Part V, Chapter I"
memes.description: "That night I didn’t get on board till just before midnight and Dominic could not conceal his relief at having me safely there."

memes.label: "Part V, Chapter II"
memes.description: "Notwithstanding my misanthropy I had to see a few people on account of all these Royalist affairs which I couldn’t very well drop, and in truth did not wish to drop."

memes.label: "Part V, Chapter III"
memes.description: "I must say that for the next three months I threw myself into my unlawful trade with a sort of desperation, dogged and hopeless, like a fairly decent fellow who takes deliberately to drink."

memes.label: "Part V, Chapter IV"
memes.description: "It was the last evening of Carnival. The same masks, the same yells, the same mad rushes, the same bedlam of disguised humanity blowing about the streets in the great gusts of mistral that seemed t..."

memes.label: "Part V, Chapter V"
memes.description: "Coming out of the bright light of the studio I didn’t make out Therese very distinctly. She, however, having groped in dark cupboards, must have had her pupils sufficiently dilated to have seen tha..."

memes.label: "Part V, Chapter VI"
memes.description: "I had a momentary suspicion that I had said something stupid. Her smile amongst many other things seemed to have meant that, too. And I answered it with a certain resignation:"

memes.label: "Part V, Chapter VII"
memes.description: "My brain was in a whirl. I am safe to say that at this precise moment there was nobody completely sane in the house."

memes.label: "Part V, Chapter VIII"
memes.description: "Directly I had shut the door after the doctor I started shouting for Therese. “Come down at once, you wretched hypocrite,” I yelled at the foot of the stairs in a sort of frenzy as though I had bee..."

memes.label: River Clyde
memes.description: River in western Scotland where Mills joined a chartered steamer carrying arms for the Carlist cause — the point of origin for the ill-fated supply mission that ended in shipwreck in the Bay of Biscay.

memes.label: Corsica
memes.description: Mediterranean island where Henry Allègre owned a house, later inherited by Doña Rita — a site of sentimental pilgrimage and the location of the balancelle Dominic procured for the gun-running scheme.

memes.label: Danger
memes.description: The pervasive atmosphere of physical and moral risk that surrounds the novel's action, from the literal dangers of gun-running to the psychological dangers of passionate attachment

memes.label: Dominic's Balancelle Voyages
memes.description: The series of clandestine Mediterranean voyages in the balancelle *Tremolino*, including the Corsica expedition to purchase the vessel and the smuggling runs supplying Carlist forces on the Spanish coast

memes.label: Dominique
memes.description: The Mediterranean sailor and loyal operative in the Carlist gun-running enterprise, a figure of romantic competence whom Conrad explicitly links to an earlier fictional creation
aliases: Dominique of The Mirror of the Sea

memes.label: Don Carlos de Bourbon
memes.description: The historical Carlist pretender to the Spanish throne whose claim provides the political cause around which the novel's action revolves — a shadowy figure who never appears directly but whose existence animates every character's commitments

memes.label: Doña Rita
memes.description: The enigmatic central female figure of the novel — a Basque woman of mysterious origins who becomes the emotional and operational center of the Carlist arms-smuggling intrigue in Marseilles

memes.label: Doña Rita at Tolosa
memes.description: Doña Rita's daring wartime crossing of the front lines to visit Carlist headquarters, one of the novel's most vivid set-pieces

memes.label: Duty
memes.description: The military and moral obligation that structures the lives of the novel's soldier-characters, providing a framework of disciplined action against which personal passions play out

memes.label: Exile
memes.description: The condition of geographical and social displacement shared by the novel's major characters, shaping their identities and enabling their involvement in transnational political intrigue

memes.label: Fate
memes.description: The sense of predetermined or impersonal forces shaping events that pervades the novel, articulated through the narrator's retrospective awareness and the characters' attempts to understand their circumstances

memes.label: Freedom
memes.description: The condition of unconstrained agency that the novel's exiled and adventurous characters both enjoy and are haunted by, presented as the ambiguous gift of displacement from conventional society

memes.label: George's First Kiss of Rita's Hand
memes.description: The first physical contact between Monsieur George and Doña Rita — at the end of George's initial visit to the Pavilion, Rita calls him back and wordlessly presses the back of her hand against his lips, transforming their formal acquaintance into an unspoken intimacy.
aliases: Rita's hand pressed to George's lips

memes.label: Guipuzcoa
memes.description: Basque province in northern Spain where Don Carlos de Bourbon launched his Carlist insurrection — the geographical crucible of the Legitimist cause that drives the novel's conspiracy.

memes.label: Gulf of Mexico
memes.description: Body of water in the western Atlantic where Monsieur George was involved in an ill-defined and vaguely illegal enterprise before the events of the novel — a marker of his youthful, restless maritime past.

memes.label: Haiti
memes.description: The Caribbean negro republic where Monsieur George spent a few days during his Gulf of Mexico wanderings — mentioned in conversation as a unique example of a black-governed state, prompting Captain Blunt's extended reflections on race.

memes.label: Henry Allègre
memes.description: The wealthy connoisseur and collector who discovered the young Doña Rita in the Basque hills, educated her, and made her his companion — his death before the novel's action leaves a legacy of wealth, art, and emotional complexity that shapes everything that follows

memes.label: Henry Allègre's Death
memes.description: The death of Henry Allègre — art collector, millionaire, and Doña Rita's protector — recounted by Rita as a traumatic watershed that cast her abruptly from a protected existence into the exposed life of "the heiress of Mr. Allègre."

memes.label: Honour
memes.description: The aristocratic code of personal integrity that governs the novel's social world, operating as both a genuine moral standard and a form of self-presentation

memes.label: Illusion
memes.description: The psychological mechanism through which characters invest political causes and personal relationships with imagined significance, a central Conradian theme of self-deception

memes.label: Intrigue
memes.description: The web of secret political operations and personal machinations that constitutes the novel's plot mechanism, blurring the line between political commitment and personal manipulation

memes.label: José Ortega
memes.description: A minor but memorable figure in the Carlist intrigue — described as "very useful" but deeply unstimulating, whose presence illuminates the more mundane side of political conspiracy

memes.label: Legitimism
memes.description: The political doctrine of hereditary dynastic right that animated the Carlist cause and defined the social world of the novel

memes.label: Lord X
memes.description: A wealthy English aristocrat, relative of Mills, who financed the Carlist arms-smuggling steamer — mentioned but never appears directly in the narrative

memes.label: Madame Léonore
memes.description: Proprietress of a quiet café on the Marseilles quay, Dominic's devoted companion, and a shrewd observer of the novel's central intrigues — her café serves as the operational headquarters for the gun-running conspiracy.

memes.label: Mademoiselle Therese
memes.description: Doña Rita's older sister, a devout peasant woman who dresses like a nun and manages Rita's house in Marseilles — a figure of religious judgment and rustic simplicity amid the novel's cosmopolitan intrigue

memes.label: Maison Dorée
memes.description: The opulent restaurant and private dining-rooms across from the Cannebière café where Prax's "infernal supper" takes place, and later the setting for José Ortega's desperate encounter with Monsieur George — a venue of carnival excess and mortal danger.

memes.label: Maritime Alps
memes.description: Mountain range on the border between France and Italy where George and Doña Rita retreated to a small stone house embowered with roses — the setting of the novel's idyllic interlude, suppressed by the narrator.

memes.label: Marquis de Villarel
memes.description: Spanish grandee — Captain General of the Royal Bodyguard at the Legitimist Court — whose political and domestic influence make him a key Carlist power-broker, and who figures in Doña Rita's childhood memories as the nobleman who lent his authority to her uncle's letter.

memes.label: Marseilles
memes.description: The French Mediterranean port city where the novel's central action unfolds — headquarters of the Carlist gun-running operation and setting for the social world of exiles and adventurers

memes.label: Mediterranean Sea
memes.description: The sea that shapes the novel's geography and identities — the arena of Dominic's maritime expertise, the setting of the gun-running operation, and a symbolic space of lawless freedom between the coasts of France, Spain, and Italy.

memes.label: Memory
memes.description: The retrospective consciousness through which the narrator frames the entire story, transforming political adventure into an act of personal excavation

memes.label: Mills's Visit to Headquarters
memes.description: Mills's journey to the Carlist Legitimist headquarters at Tolosa to liaise with the military command, assess the state of the campaign, and report back to Doña Rita on the conduct of the war.

memes.label: Monsieur George
memes.description: The young first-person narrator and protagonist, whose involvement in Carlist gun-running in Marseilles forms the novel's central experience of initiation into passion and adventure

memes.label: Monsieur Mills
memes.description: The calm, experienced Englishman who serves as mentor to the young protagonist, introducing him to the Carlist intrigue and to Doña Rita's world

memes.label: Old Harbour
memes.description: The ancient port of Marseilles — the novel's threshold space of arrivals and departures, where ships emerge from the dawn mist, conspirators return from illicit expeditions, and the boundary between lawful city and unlawful sea dissolves.

memes.label: Old Town (Marseilles)
memes.description: The old harbour quarter of Marseilles where Monsieur George cultivated his bohemian social circle of pilots, coasters, and sailors — the human network that made him the ideal recruit for the Carlist gun-running conspiracy.

memes.label: Ortega's Attack
memes.description: José Ortega's violent intrusion at the Pavilion on the last night of Carnival, where he threatens Doña Rita with a dagger and is subdued by Monsieur George in the fencing-room

memes.label: Paris
memes.description: The French capital where legitimist sympathies and political connections extend the Carlist network beyond the Mediterranean

memes.label: Passion
memes.description: The intense emotional force that drives the novel's central relationship and its political intrigues, presented as both a vitalizing and potentially destructive power

memes.label: Passy
memes.description: Quiet residential quarter in the 16th arrondissement of Paris, site of Henry Allègre's mysterious Pavilion — the secluded house where Doña Rita presided over her salon of artists, politicians, and Legitimist conspirators.

memes.label: Place of the Opera
memes.description: The square before the Marseilles opera house — a liminal space of performance, disguise, and nocturnal transit that bookends the narrator's secretive passage through the city on the night of the Carlist rendezvous.

memes.label: Portrait of Rita
memes.description: The painted portrait of Doña Rita that figures in the novel's meditation on beauty, representation, and the gap between image and reality

memes.label: Prax
memes.description: The sculptor who leads Monsieur George's bohemian circle on the Cannebière — he who bestowed the nickname "Young Ulysses" and whose El Greco painting hangs as a symbol of artistic discernment amid the novel's political intrigues.

memes.label: Pyrenees
memes.description: Mountain range forming the natural border between France and Spain, across which the Carlist war raged — a geographical symbol of the political and psychological divide that structures the novel's world.

memes.label: Doña Rita's Circle
memes.description: The informal network of exiles, adventurers, artists, and legitimist sympathizers centered on Doña Rita's Marseilles salon — the social infrastructure of the Carlist intrigue

memes.label: Rita's Flight from Paris
memes.description: Doña Rita's departure from the Pavilion in Paris following the personal crisis with Captain Blunt and Ortega, and George's anguished leave-taking before sailing for his gun-running mission.

memes.label: Romance
memes.description: The literary mode and thematic concern with idealized love, adventure, and the tension between illusion and reality that pervades the novel

memes.label: Rose
memes.description: Doña Rita's loyal maid and confidante, a steady domestic presence whose devotion to her mistress provides a counterpoint to the novel's world of political intrigue and romantic turbulence

memes.label: South Carolina
memes.description: The American origin of Captain Blunt — a family plantation in pre-Civil War South Carolina that shapes his ambiguous identity as a Southern gentleman adrift in European Legitimist circles.

memes.label: Street of the Consuls
memes.description: The narrow Marseilles street where Doña Rita's town house stands — a dim, quiet street of ancient stone that conceals the novel's central romantic and political intrigues behind its unremarkable façade.

memes.label: The Arms Smuggling Operation
memes.description: The clandestine operation to salvage stranded rifles and deliver them to Carlist forces in Spain — the novel's central plot mechanism

memes.label: The Arrow of Gold
memes.description: The novel's title object — a symbolic token whose significance resonates throughout the narrative as an emblem of value, desire, and the ungraspable

memes.label: The Balcony Scene
memes.description: George's recurring nighttime returns to the street of the Consuls, where his vigil below Doña Rita's windows becomes a ritual of longing, self-examination, and the impossible distance between the world of action and the world of desire.
aliases: the street of the Consuls at night

memes.label: Madame Léonore's Café
memes.description: Small, quiet café on the Marseilles quay kept by Madame Léonore — Dominic's headquarters, the operational centre of the Carlist maritime conspiracy, and the setting of key nocturnal confabulations between George and Dominic.

memes.label: The Carnival Meeting
memes.description: Monsieur George's fateful encounter with Mills and Captain Blunt at a café on the Cannebière during the last evening of Carnival, leading to his recruitment into the Carlist conspiracy

memes.label: The Gun-Running Operation
memes.description: The Carlist arms-smuggling enterprise across the Mediterranean, organized by Monsieur George with the seaman Dominic using the balancelle *Tremolino*, supplying munitions to the Legitimist forces in Spain

memes.label: The Pavilion
memes.description: Doña Rita's residence in Marseilles — the physical and symbolic center of the novel's social world

memes.label: The Prado
memes.description: The fashionable promenade and residential district of Marseilles where the Villa — Doña Rita's seaside residence and Allègre's former home — commands its view of the Mediterranean, serving as the novel's primary stage for romantic encounter and revelation.

memes.label: The Shipwreck
memes.description: The gun-running steamer chased ashore by the Republican ironclad Numancia — the originating adventure that connects Mills to the Carlist cause

memes.label: The Tremolino
memes.description: The fast Mediterranean balancelle sailing craft purchased in Corsica for Carlist gun-running — the unnamed vessel in which Monsieur George "plied his fantastic trade," named by Conrad in the Author's Note as the Tremolino, whose wrecking George experiences as a symbolic death of his former self.

memes.label: Therese
memes.description: Doña Rita's older sister, a figure of conventional morality whose disapproval of Rita's life introduces a voice of judgment into the novel's world of intrigue and passion

memes.label: Tolosa
memes.description: The Carlist headquarters town in northern Spain where Doña Rita makes her daring wartime visit

memes.label: West Indies
memes.description: Tropical maritime region where the narrator George served on two voyages before the novel's events — a recurring reference point symbolizing his former life of adventure, his ongoing economic ties, and an escape route he contemplates but never takes.

memes.label: Youth
memes.description: The period of life that the novel both narrates and elegizes, presented as the enabling condition for adventure, romance, and self-deception
